name: Devtools cold intro
icp: Engineering manager / staff engineer at series A–C startups
body:
Hi {{firstName}},

I saw {{specificObservation}} — that resonated because most teams I talk to are dealing with {{painPoint}} around the same growth stage.

I'm building {{productName}}. It's a {{oneLineDescription}}. Engineering teams at {{customerExampleA}} and {{customerExampleB}} use it to cut {{painPoint}} cycles roughly in half.

Not trying to pitch a platform. If it's relevant, I can share a 5-minute Loom of how a team your size set it up — no demo call needed. If you'd rather just try it, the docs are public and there's a free tier with no card.

Worth a look?

{{senderFirstName}}

variables.note: Something concrete: a recent post, a job listing, a talk, a repo. Avoid 'I saw your company is growing fast.'
The narrow problem your product solves, in their words. e.g. 'flaky CI', 'slow PR reviews'.
One sentence, no jargon. e.g. 'CI cache layer that cuts test times by skipping unchanged files.'
A recognizable customer name if you have permission to share.

notes: Works because it leads with a specific observation, names the pain in their language, and removes the friction of a demo call by offering a Loom. Skip the customer logos line if you don't have permission to use names. The free-tier mention only works if you actually have a free tier; otherwise drop that sentence.
